授業目的: Canada and Business in Movies introduces students to a multiplicity of Canadian perspectives on business and industry. Topics treated include the industries of communications, housing, mobility, and reproduction. Students may help us decide what texts we will treat in class. Students who wish to improve their English are very welcome.
到達目標: Students will have familiarized themselves with some of the important Canadian filmic commentaries on business. Students will have improved their critical reading, viewing and thinking skills. Students will have increased their capacity for active learning. Students will have practiced task-based, group learning and presentation skills. Students will have improved their English reading, writing, and speaking skills.
授業方法: Face-to-Face (12 sessions) This course will combine Lecture, Seminar, Discussion, Presentation format

第1回:
Session 1) 【Startup Week (including It Changed Everything】《90 Minutes》

第6回: Session 6) 【Bikes vs. Cars and Student Present】《90 Minutes》
第7回: Session 7) 【Not Just Bikes: These Two Cities Used to be the Same and Visited the Best* City in North America (Montréal and Student Present】《90 Minutes》
第8回: Session 8) 【Bicicles – The Winter Cycling Revolution and Not Just Bikes: Why Canadians Can't Bike in the Winter (but Finnish people can): 】  《90 Minutes》
トピック.授業外学習2: Students should ideally watch the film prior to the class session. They are to write thought journals about the film of 100-250 words in total and one discussion question about it before or after class and bring them to class session. We will watch key parts in class and discuss. Flex sessions are to be used to prepare group presentations about a topic covered in class and will also serve to review for the in-class test. The instructor is pleased to tailor this course to the needs of the students.

教科書/Required texts0: These films are available on the internet, many at Canada Broadcasting Service GEM series. Many are free. Some are available on streaming services for a small fee.
